Children.Text: VeloxDev provides .NET developers with the complete architecture required to build interactive workflow editors.

### Adaptation Library Out-of-the-box, bound to the GUI framework. If you need agent or serialization capabilities, you can additionally install VeloxDev.Core.Extension.

| Platform | Package | NuGet |
|----------|---------|-------|
| WPF | `VeloxDev.WPF` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.WPF?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.WPF/) |
| Avalonia | `VeloxDev.Avalonia` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.Avalonia?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.Avalonia/) |
| WinUI | `VeloxDev.WinUI` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.WinUI?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.WinUI/) |
| MAUI | `VeloxDev.MAUI` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.MAUI?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.MAUI/) |
| WinForms | `VeloxDev.WinForms` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.WinForms?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.WinForms/) |
| Razor | `VeloxDev.Razor` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.Razor?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.Razor/) |

###  Core Library

> Independent of any GUI framework, optional installation; their upgrades may be faster than those of the adaptation libraries, and sometimes they need to be installed or upgraded separately.

| Package | NuGet | Description |
|---------|-------|-------------|
| `VeloxDev.Core` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.Core?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.Core/) | Workflow abstractions, MVVM generators, and runtime models — zero third-party dependencies |
| `VeloxDev.Core.Extension` | [![NuGet](https://img.shields.io/nuget/v/VeloxDev.Core.Extension?color=4caf50&logo=nuget)](https://www.nuget.org/packages/VeloxDev.Core.Extension/) | MAF-based Workflow Agent tools and additional runtime extensions |
Flip through this section to quickly learn about historically important version updates. The document reduction rules are as follows:

Cells: Modify the usage of existing function blocks
Create a subpage in the 【Version】 section to describe new content, and mark the version of the change in the corresponding original text

Children.Text: Summary: Agent building paradigm upgrade & increased ways to create branch nodes

Agents can now trigger permission confirmation dialogs and radio buttons, marking an important milestone that enables you to orchestrate agent tasks in a more controlled manner. Additionally, the workspace now includes a new set of practical configuration methods, including global language configuration and automatic scanning of workflow context from assemblies.

Children.Code: Console.WriteLine("Hello VeloxDev");

Children.Text: First, there is a new interface definition. When you want to use a custom type to create a branch with multiple connectors, you can implement the ISlotProvider interface.
Thus, you can easily implement a conditional provider for presenting multiple connectors at once.

Finally, when you want to have custom conditional routing inside a Node component, you can use the previously defined CustomRouteSelector to dynamically generate it. Once defined, the agent will understand the construction of CustomRouteSelector based on serialization, and by creating and passing instance objects, it enables the generation of dynamic connectors.
Summary: Item template support, SlotEnumerator improvements, MAUI attached property improvements
Summary: prompt optimization, upgrade of aspect-oriented programming approach, remove outdated AOT implementation (.NET built-in mechanism is already the best)

The source generator no longer generates a property named Proxy for view models, instead adds extension methods and global cache management while preserving dynamic creation of original interfaces. This is to completely eliminate potential inheritance chain break risk. The API has undergone a major change.
The IProxy interface has been renamed to avoid ambiguity with concepts such as network proxies.
Built-in weak reference dictionary. When accessing a proxy via Aop(), it will query the dictionary cache; if it does not exist, it will be automatically constructed.
In very rare cases, it may be necessary to reverse lookup the proxy target of a proxy object; the return value is nullable.
Focus on practical application and master the usage of the project.
VeloxDev provides distinctive ViewModel completion and Command generation, is compatible with mainstream MVVM frameworks in the community, and avoids regenerating infrastructure.

Children.Code: [VeloxProperty] private string name = string.Empty;
[VeloxProperty] public partial string Name { get; protected set; }

Children.Text: If a member derives from INotifyCollectionChanged, additional callback functions are provided. Specifically, when the value of a notification property changes, the subscription and disposal of callback functions are handled internally. It is worth noting that partial properties cannot declare an initial value at definition time, so it is recommended to initialize these collections during construction.
The following code will ultimately generate SaveCommand, regardless of whether it carries the ”Async“ suffix.

No matter how you execute a command, the essence is waiting for a Task, so in most cases there is no need to worry that calling Execute instead of ExecuteAsync will block the UI; they are almost equivalent.

Children.Code:
SaveCommand.Execute(null);

await SaveCommand.ExecuteAsync(null);

Children.Text: The cancellation of the command also provides two versions; here demonstrates the awaitable version.
When you set canValidate: true, the CanExecuteSaveCommand callback will be generated internally. You can determine whether to allow command execution in the callback function.

You can manually notify the UI to re-evaluate the executability of the command, at which point the parameter is null. If the command is executed, at this point the parameter is the parameter received by the command.

Children.Code: SaveCommand.Notify();

Children.Text: You can monitor the total number of currently executing tasks, handle exceptions, and perform other operations. These callback events all carry a CommandEventArgs parameter.
💡V5.4.0 has undergone API changes. See the [Version] section.

.NET5+ natively supports proxy-based aspect-oriented programming, meaning that without modifying the source code of types, you can achieve dynamic interception and customize the behavior of properties and methods. VeloxDev has provided source code generation for it.
When setting up a proxy, there are five key parameters.
Use these parameters in the SetProxy method.

Cells: Parameters received at the entry point, such as the value keyword in properties or parameters obtained during method calls
Parameters returned from the previous lifecycle

Children.Text: To make hooks effective, properties must be accessed through a proxy. In actual business scenarios, you can choose as needed.
Frame loop behavior is common in scenarios such as physics simulation and state monitoring. Now, you can build it using a feature.
You can create multiple groups based on the channel parameter, and you can use the fps parameter to specify the refresh rate.
Basic control can be implemented based on these static functions.

Children.Code:
MonoBehaviourManager.Start("game"); // start

MonoBehaviourManager.Pause("game"); // pause (not equal to stop)

MonoBehaviourManager.Resume("game"); // resume

await MonoBehaviourManager.StopAsync("game"); // stop

Children.Text: You can modify the time multiplier and refresh rate at any time.

Children.Code:
MonoBehaviourManager.SetTimeScale(0.5f, "game"); // time scale

MonoBehaviourManager.SetTargetFPS(30, "game"); // frame rate

bool isRun = MonoBehaviourManager.IsRunning("game")；

bool isPaused = MonoBehaviourManager.IsPaused("game")；

Cells: Distance from the previous frame's time

Children.Text: Almost any GUI framework has its own dedicated interfaces for animation handling, which makes the creation of interpolation animations depend to some extent on the GUI's syntax design, and there may be limitations. Taking WPF as an example: standard animations must be built based on DependencyProperty. Although this is intended to facilitate internal performance optimization, it also sacrifices flexibility.

VeloxDev advocates compatibility, internally designing a complete abstraction layer from animation construction to execution. Each GUI framework requires only a small amount of adaptation code. Ultimately, animations act on standard properties rather than any GUI-specific objects.
Theme - Implementing Vivid Theme Color Switching
You can build animations using the same API in any supported GUI framework. We provide the most elegant approach so far, with an emphasis on type safety and thread safety.
In the following example, you will create an animation to modify the horizontal offset and fill color of a Rectangle element.
The animation process can be split into multiple segments, then linked together in sequence.

Children.Code:
Transition<Rectangle>.Create()
    .Property()
    .Effect()
    .Then()  // Chain, execute the next animation immediately
    .Property()
    .Effect()
    .AwaitThen() // Chain, wait for a specific time before executing the next animation
    .Property()
    .Effect();

Children.Text: Use the following code to control the animation toggle.

Children.Code:
animation.Execute(rect); // start, animation applies to the rect instance

Transition.Exit(rect); // terminate

Children.Text: The start of the animation is thread-safe, so there is no need to worry about the following scenario.

The IInterpolable interface allows types to directly participate in interpolation. By implementing this interface, you can directly use custom types in the Property function when building animations with Fluent.
IEaseCalculator interface allows you to customize easing effects.

Animation is based on interpolation between two states, and the snapshot mechanism is one of its implementations. VeloxDev has exposed it, so you can also use this mechanism to create animations, or for state saving and restoration (only used in animation scenarios).

Children.Code:
// Essentially still an animation, configurable effects
snapshot.Effect(TransitionEffects.Empty).Execute(view);

Children.Text: This is a branching capability extended from the animation system, which can naturally transition from one set of theme colors to another in the UI.
If you configure an interpolator, the following functions will become available.
Of course, considering that gradient-style switching may cause performance issues in some scenarios, a way to not load animations is also provided, and this method is supported at all times.

Process and automation are common in modern software, especially in business pipelines and device control flow scenarios. Now, you can elegantly build easily extensible runtime workflows in .NET. As artificial intelligence advances, it is no longer far off for agents to understand and manipulate in-memory objects. What you need is creativity and approval, rather than writing large amounts of code.
Avalonia / WPF / WinUI / MAUI / Winforms have all implemented the following effect
Based on MVVM, no GUI dependencies

You can draw interactive workflow orchestration views in your favorite GUI framework, or run the orchestrated workflows on a server without a UI. VeloxDev.Core.Extension provides persistence services to support sharing the same data across these modes.
Implement spatial indexing and querying of nodes based on the grid hash algorithm, achieving microsecond-level response in measured environments.
The workflow framework has deeply integrated the Function Calling capability provided by MAF. Your agent can now understand and manipulate workflows at runtime.
WorkflowBuilder provides source code generation capabilities; just annotate this feature to implement the workflow component kernel without inheritance.

WorkflowHelper provides the logic code required by workflow components. They are injected into the view model during the generation phase and support dynamic replacement, enabling a highly extensible architecture by overriding Helper's built-in functions and subscribing to Helper's built-in events.
A workflow is a graph structure that contains four important components. This chapter will show you how to build the view model corresponding to the components.

Cells: Global container, stores all data of a single workflow
Node, carries configuration and business execution
Connector, carries connection relationships and channel direction restrictions
Connection line, can access the Slot of the start and end points

Children.Text: Next, all view models will be built based on the annotated characteristics described below: $component is the component type, and $helper is the injected business logic.

Children.Code: 【WorkflowBuilder.{$component}<{$helper}>】

Children.Text:
| Member                                               | Type                                                                                               | Description                     |
| -------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `VirtualLink`                                    | `IWorkflowLinkViewModel`                                                                         | Virtual connection during connection establishment |
| `Nodes`                                          | `ObservableCollection<IWorkflowNodeViewModel>`                                                   | All Node components           |
| `Links`                                          | `ObservableCollection<IWorkflowLinkViewModel>`                                                   | All Link components           |
|`Layout`                                          | 'CanvasLayout'                                                                                                            | Canvas layout                 |

| Member                                               | Type                                                                                               | Description                     |
| -------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `CreateNodeCommand`                              | `IVeloxCommand`                                                                                  | Create node command             |
| `SetPointerCommand`                              | `IVeloxCommand`                                                                                  | Update pointer position command         |
| `ResetVirtualLinkCommand`                        | `IVeloxCommand`                                                                                  | Reset virtual link command         |
| `RedoCommand`                                    | `IVeloxCommand`                                                                                  | Redo command                 |
| `UndoCommand`                                    | `IVeloxCommand`                                                                                  | Undo command                 |

Node view model in the definition phase has two special members:

- `IWorkflowSlotViewModel` derived classes must be marked with `[VeloxProperty]`
- `SlotEnumerator<T>` can use bool or enum as condition branches, generating multiple slots based on conditions inside the collection, and must also be marked with `[VeloxProperty]`

As a result, you can directly render multiple connectors from the `InputSlot` property + `OutputSlots` collection in the view later. Note that at this point, the UI is responsible for tracking the slot's coordinates (Anchor) and passing them back to the view model. If the UI lacks such coordinate tracking, the start and end positions of your `Link` component cannot be calculated correctly.

SlotEnumerator<T> implements the IEnumerable interface. The child elements are defined as follows: Name is the name of the enum member, Value is the enum value, Slot is the automatically constructed Slot component.

| Member | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `Parent` | `IWorkflowTreeViewModel?` | Parent tree |
| `Anchor` | `Anchor` | Node anchor coordinates |
| `Size` | `Size` | Node size |
| `Slots` | `ObservableCollection<IWorkflowSlotViewModel>` | Slot collection owned by the node |

| Member | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `MoveCommand` | `IVeloxCommand` | Move node command |
| `SetAnchorCommand` | `IVeloxCommand` | Set anchor command |
| `SetSizeCommand` | `IVeloxCommand` | Set size command |
| `CreateSlotCommand` | `IVeloxCommand` | Create Slot command |
| `DeleteCommand` | `IVeloxCommand` | Delete node command |
| `WorkCommand` | `IVeloxCommand` | Execute work command |
| `BroadcastCommand` | `IVeloxCommand` | Forward broadcast command |
| `ReverseBroadcastCommand` | `IVeloxCommand` | Reverse broadcast command |

| Member                                               | Type                                             | Description                      |
| ---------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------- |
| `Targets`                                          | `ObservableCollection<IWorkflowSlotViewModel>` | Target collection of the current Slot   |
| `Sources`                                          | `ObservableCollection<IWorkflowSlotViewModel>` | Source collection received by the current Slot |
| `Parent`                                           | `IWorkflowNodeViewModel?`                       | Parent Node                     |
| `Channel`                                          | `SlotChannel`                                   | Slot channel type               |
| `State`                                            | `SlotState`                                     | Slot connection state           |
| `Anchor`                                           | `Anchor`                                        | Slot anchor position            |

| Member                                               | Type                                             | Description                  |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------- |
| `SetChannelCommand`                              | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Set channel command               |
| `SendConnectionCommand`                          | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Send connection command               |
| `ReceiveConnectionCommand`                       | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Receive connection command               |
| `DeleteCommand`                                  | `IVeloxCommand`                                | Delete Slot command             |

| 成员                                               | 类型                             | Description            |
| -------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | --------------- |
| `Sender`                                         | `IWorkflowSlotViewModel`       | Connection initiator      |
| `Receiver`                                       | `IWorkflowSlotViewModel`       | Connection receiver      |
| `IsVisible`                                      | `bool`                         | Whether the connection is visible    |

| Member | Type | Description |
| ------ | ---- | ----------- |
| `DeleteCommand` | `IVeloxCommand` | Delete connection command |

All components in the workflow have injectable and replaceable logic, and provide events and overridable methods for customization. Each workflow component you define holds a Helper property that allows you to access the business implementation.

After using the generic version, all built-in Component properties of Helper will transform into specific derived classes, while the Component properties inside the non-generic version are in the form of interface abstract.

Cells: Provides extended behavior for the global container

VeloxDev has provided item template support for some platforms that support additional behaviors. Using them can efficiently build workflow views. This chapter demonstrates the basic command line usage and template code fine-tuning points. Details about additional attributes can be found in the next section.
Install - {$plateform} optional Avalonia / WPF / WinUI / MAUI

Children.Code: dotnet new install VeloxDev.{$plateform}.Templates

Children.Text: Create - {$plateform} Optional ava / wpf / winui / maui

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-tree -n TreeView -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: For the item template of the Tree component, it is often necessary to modify the mapping of ViewModel → View. Generally, for Avalonia, you do not need to write a template selector, but other platforms must create one.

Children.Code:
<!-- VeloxDev customization: Generate the Node, Slot, Link, selector, and grid-decorator templates, then update the local type names below if you renamed them. -->
<UserControl xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
			 xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
			 xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/expression/blend/2008"
			 xmlns:mc="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/markup-compatibility/2006"
			 xmlns:local="using:TemplateNamespace"
			 mc:Ignorable="d" d:DesignWidth="800" d:DesignHeight="450"
			 x:Class="TemplateNamespace.TemplateClass"
			 xmlns:behaviors="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem.AttachedBehaviors"
			 xmlns:framework="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem"
			 behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.IsEnabled="True" behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.ScrollViewerName="PART_ScrollViewer" behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.CanvasName="PART_Canvas" behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.GridDecoratorName="PART_GridDecorator" behaviors:WorkflowSurfaceBehavior.PointerPressSourceName="PART_SurfaceBorder"
			 x:CompileBindings="False">
			 <!-- Replace x:CompileBindings=False with a concrete x:DataType after defining your ViewModel. -->
	
	<UserControl.DataTemplates>
		<!-- Node Templates -->
		<DataTemplate DataType="framework:IWorkflowNodeViewModel">
			<local:NodeView Width="{Binding Size.Width}" Height="{Binding Size.Height}" Canvas.Left="{Binding Anchor.Horizontal}" Canvas.Top="{Binding Anchor.Vertical}" ZIndex="{Binding Anchor.Layer}" RenderTransform="{Binding $parent[local:TemplateClass].(behaviors:WorkflowCanvasTransformBehavior.Transform)}" />
		</DataTemplate>

		<!-- Connection Templates -->
		<DataTemplate DataType="framework:IWorkflowLinkViewModel">
			<local:LinkView StartLeft="{Binding Sender.Anchor.Horizontal}" StartTop="{Binding Sender.Anchor.Vertical}" EndLeft="{Binding Receiver.Anchor.Horizontal}" EndTop="{Binding Receiver.Anchor.Vertical}" ZIndex="-1" CanRender="{Binding IsVisible}" Width="{Binding $parent[Canvas].Bounds.Width}" Height="{Binding $parent[Canvas].Bounds.Height}" RenderTransform="{Binding $parent[local:TemplateClass].(behaviors:WorkflowCanvasTransformBehavior.Transform)}" />
		</DataTemplate>
	</UserControl.DataTemplates>

</UserControl>

Children.Text: Create - {$plateform} optional ava / wpf / winui / maui

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-node -n NodeView -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: For the item template of the Node component, it is often necessary to modify the rendering method of its internal Slot members. As mentioned earlier in the documentation, the view model of the Node component can define a single Slot or a collection of multiple Slots in two ways. In XAML, their Name must be passed to the corresponding attached properties, namely SlotNames and SlotEnumeratorNames, both of which can use commas to separate multiple members.

Children.Code:
<!-- Customize the content, but keep PART_* names synchronized with WorkflowSlotLayoutBehavior. -->
<UserControl xmlns="https://github.com/avaloniaui"
			 xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2006/xaml"
			 xmlns:d="http://schemas.microsoft.com/expression/blend/2008"
			 xmlns:mc="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/markup-compatibility/2006"
			 xmlns:local="using:TemplateNamespace"
			 xmlns:behaviors="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem.AttachedBehaviors"
			 mc:Ignorable="d" d:DesignWidth="800" d:DesignHeight="450"
			 x:Class="TemplateNamespace.TemplateClass"
			 behaviors:WorkflowSlotLayoutBehavior.IsEnabled="True" behaviors:WorkflowSlotLayoutBehavior.SlotNames="PART_InputSlot" behaviors:WorkflowSlotLayoutBehavior.SlotEnumeratorNames="PART_OutputSlots" behaviors:WorkflowSlotLayoutBehavior.CoordinateHostName="PART_Canvas"
			 Foreground="TemplateNodeForeground"
			 x:CompileBindings="False">
			 <!-- Replace x:CompileBindings=False with a concrete x:DataType after defining your ViewModel. -->

        <!-- Node drag behavior -->
		<Grid behaviors:WorkflowNodeDragBehavior.CoordinateHostName="PART_Canvas" behaviors:WorkflowNodeDragBehavior.IsEnabled="True"
			  Grid.Row="0"
			  Margin="12,0">
		</Grid>
        
        <!-- Single Slot component -->
		<local:SlotView x:Name="PART_InputSlot" DataContext="{Binding InputSlot}"
						Width="18"
						Height="18"
						Margin="2,0,0,0"
						HorizontalAlignment="Left"
						VerticalAlignment="Center"
						Foreground="TemplateNodeForeground" />
     
        <!-- Multiple Slot components -->
		<ItemsControl x:Name="PART_OutputSlots" ItemsSource="{Binding OutputSlots.Items}"
					  Grid.Row="1"
					  Margin="12,6"
					  VerticalAlignment="Center">
			<ItemsControl.ItemTemplate>
				<DataTemplate>
					<Grid ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto" Margin="0,3" ClipToBounds="False">
						<TextBlock Text="{Binding Name}"
								   Margin="0,0,10,0"
								   HorizontalAlignment="Right"
								   VerticalAlignment="Center" />
						<local:SlotView DataContext="{Binding Slot}"
												Grid.Column="1"
												Width="14"
												Height="14"
												Margin="0"
												Foreground="TemplateNodeForeground" />
					</Grid>
				</DataTemplate>
			</ItemsControl.ItemTemplate>
		</ItemsControl>

</UserControl>

Children.Text: Create - {$plateform} Options: ava / wpf / winui / maui

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-slot -n SlotView -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: Create - {$plateform} Optional ava / wpf / winui / maui

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-link -n LinkView -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: Create - {$plateform} optional: ava / wpf / winui / maui

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-decorator -n GridDecorator -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: The grid decorator exchanges data with the adapter based on the following interface. When the user interacts, the four properties here will be updated when necessary, and you only need to focus on the decorator's own drawing logic.
Create - {$plateform} optional ava / wpf / winui / maui, where ava can skip using this template.

Children.Code: dotnet new {$plateform}-v-selector -n ComponentSelector -ns MyApp.Views

Children.Text: If you have already read about component construction and business logic injection, then now the workflow can run.
The problem solved in this section is: how to implement a workflow GUI based on additional behaviors.

This chapter is applicable to Avalonia / WPF / WinUI / MAUI (Winforms does not have an attached property mechanism, only the API is similar; the recommended practice is to directly transplant from the demo in the repository; Razor is still in the experimental stage).

Children.Code: <UserControl xmlns:behaviors="using:VeloxDev.WorkflowSystem.AttachedBehaviors" />

Children.Text: Provide behavior registration based on Name capture at the UserControl level, covering the interactive behaviors required by elements such as Canvas, ScrollViewer, and grid decoration lines.

| Additional Property | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsEnabled` | `bool` | Enable the entire workflow canvas interaction behavior |
| `ScrollViewerName` | `string?` | Specify the `ScrollViewer` used for scrolling and viewport calculations |
| `CanvasName` | `string?` | Specify the workflow content canvas |
| `GridDecoratorName` | `string?` | Specify the grid/ruler decorator to synchronize scroll offset and content offset |
| `PointerPressSourceName` | `string?` | Specify the element that receives press events to initiate canvas panning |
Providing an object pool mechanism for Canvas, reusing view elements plays a key role in reducing GC pressure and improving rendering performance.

Cells: Depends on the GUI framework's corresponding template selector interface

Children.Text: The node mounts connectors, and this set of additional attributes is responsible for the behavior of these connectors.

When dragging, performs offset coordinate detection based on a host container

Children.Text: Responsible for injecting the connector with the behavior of initiating and receiving connections.

VeloxDev.Core.Extension provides a set of extension methods for view models, with the following features:
    - Dictionary<,> can use any type as Key, no longer limited to string
    - Only when both the setter and getter of a property are public, the property will be serialized, and serialization only processes properties

| Target/Scenario | Configuration Writing | Why This Configuration | Recommended? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Debugging, viewing JSON, making diffs | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithIndented().WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto).WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | JSON has the best readability and fully preserves polymorphism, null values, and default values; most suitable for troubleshooting workflow restoration issues | Strongly Recommended |
| Formal save to file | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithCompact().WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto).WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | Smaller file size but still retains all information needed for recovery; suitable for workflow persistence | Recommended |
| Pursuing minimal size only | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithCompact().WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Ignore).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Ignore)` | Can reduce JSON size, but may lose fields needed for recovery; not suitable for complex workflow object graphs | Use with caution |
| Has derived nodes, interface properties, polymorphic objects | `WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto)` | Enables recovery of actual runtime types during deserialization; typically required in Workflow scenarios | Mandatory |
| Want the state to be exactly the same after recovery | `WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | Preserves `null` and default values to avoid state deviation after deserialization | Recommended |
| Just want JSON to be more readable | `WithIndented()` | Only improves readability, does not change object semantics | On demand |
| Just want to reduce file size | `WithCompact()` | Removes indentation and whitespace; simplest compression method | On demand |
| Using synchronous API | `var json = tree.Serialize(options);` / `var tree = json.Deserialize<TreeViewModel>(options);` | Suitable for simple save/load | Recommended |
| Safe loading | `json.TryDeserialize<TreeViewModel>(options, out var tree)` | Safer when external files or user input may be corrupted; does not throw exceptions directly | Strongly Recommended |
| Using asynchronous API | `await tree.SerializeAsync(options)` / `await json.DeserializeAsync<TreeViewModel>(options)` | Suitable for UI, commands, file I/O chains | Recommended |
| Using streaming/Stream API | `await tree.SerializeToStreamAsync(stream, options)` / `await stream.DeserializeFromStreamAsync<TreeViewModel>(options)` | Suitable for file streams, network streams, memory streams | Recommended |
| Most reliable Workflow default scheme | `SerializationOptions.Create().WithIndented().WithTypeNameHandling(TypeNameHandling.Auto).WithNullValueHandling(NullValueHandling.Include).WithDefaultValueHandling(DefaultValueHandling.Include)` | If you are unsure which to choose, use this set; safest for workflow object graphs | Default first choice |
This chapter continuously tracks the use of VeloxDev in different GUI frameworks, collecting and answering questions.
To ensure thread safety, when using VeloxDev.WinUI, you must let UIThreadInspector obtain the window.

VeloxDev.Core.Extension provides serialization that depends on reflection, and some capabilities of the agent depend on serialization. If you encounter an inability to run the Agent or perform serialization in a trimming-sensitive environment, you can try disabling trimming.

Children.Code: <PublishTrimmed>false</PublishTrimmed>

Children.Text: Since not all runtimes support Thread, the frame loop will take two paths. To be conservative, versions below .NET 5 default to using asynchronous rather than Thread, while higher versions have built-in platform detection, whose logic follows the table below.

MonoBehaviourManager has a static property called UseAsyncLoop. When set to true, it enables asynchronous operation for maximum compatibility. You can modify UseAsyncLoop before calling the Start() function provided by this class.

Check your component mounting order: the Node component must be mounted to the Tree component before internal commands can be executed, and the Slot component must be mounted to the Node component before internal commands can be executed.
